verdict_reasoning: HSPD1/HSP60 is a mitochondrial chaperone with ~80% mitochondrial residence at baseline. However, extensive literature documents surface translocation under specific conditions: (1) apoptosis (early and late stages, loss of mitochondrial potential); (2) cellular stress (osmotic, chemical); (3) cancer cell ecto-presentation via active secretion and exosomal release; (4) terminal megakaryocyte differentiation. Surface HSP60 occurs via translocation from cytoplasm to membrane blebs or via active secretion. While the canonical form is intramitochondrial, the non-baseline ecto-pool is documented across multiple cell types and conditions.
